Corn___Common_Rust.description: A fungal disease that forms rust-colored pustules on leaves and can reduce yield when severe.
symptoms: Small elongated pustules on both leaf surfaces
Early leaf senescence in severe infections
treatment: Apply labeled fungicides when disease pressure is high
Monitor field regularly during humid conditions
Follow local agronomy guidelines for spray timing

Corn___Gray_Leaf_Spot.description: A fungal leaf disease that creates rectangular lesions and reduces photosynthetic area.
symptoms: Long rectangular gray to tan lesions
treatment: Apply recommended fungicide where economically justified
Scout early and repeat as needed under high pressure

Corn___Healthy.description: No visible disease symptoms detected on the sampled leaf.
symptoms: No necrotic lesions or fungal growth
prevention: Follow balanced fertilization and irrigation practices

Corn___Northern_Leaf_Blight.description: A fungal disease causing long cigar-shaped lesions that can significantly impact grain fill.
symptoms: Lesions turn tan as disease progresses
Rapid spread under cool humid weather
treatment: Use labeled fungicides during susceptible growth stages

Potato___Early_Blight.description: A fungal disease characterized by concentric ring lesions on older leaves.
treatment: Remove heavily infected foliage where feasible
prevention: Rotate crops and avoid continuous potato planting

Potato___Healthy.description: No visible signs of potato leaf disease are present.

Potato___Late_Blight.description: A highly destructive oomycete disease that spreads quickly in cool, wet weather.
symptoms: White fungal growth under humid conditions
treatment: Destroy infected plants to reduce spread
prevention: Avoid overhead irrigation late in the day

Rice___Brown_Spot.description: A fungal disease producing brown lesions that can lower grain quality and yield.
symptoms: Circular to oval brown spots on leaves
Gray centers with dark margins in older lesions
Seedling vigor reduction under severe infection
treatment: Use approved fungicides based on field threshold

Rice___Healthy.description: No disease-like lesions detected on rice leaves.
symptoms: No necrotic spots or blight areas

Rice___Leaf_Blast.description: A major fungal disease causing spindle-shaped lesions and serious productivity loss.

Rice___Neck_Blast.description: Blast infection at panicle neck leading to poor grain filling and white heads.
symptoms: Panicle drying and incomplete grain filling
White, empty panicles in severe cases
treatment: Spray appropriate fungicides at booting and heading stages

sugarcane___Bacterial_Blight.description: A bacterial disease causing leaf streaking and blight symptoms that reduce cane vigor.
treatment: Remove and destroy severely affected leaves/plants
Apply bactericide only where locally recommended
prevention: Avoid mechanical injury during field operations

sugarcane___Healthy.description: No visible disease symptoms detected on sugarcane leaf tissue.
symptoms: No streaking, spots, or rot lesions

sugarcane___Red_Rot.description: A destructive fungal disease often associated with reddening of internal cane tissue and leaf symptoms.
symptoms: Leaf yellowing and drying from top
Internal red discoloration with white patches in stalks
treatment: Use recommended fungicidal sett treatment before planting

Wheat___Brown_Rust.description: A fungal rust disease producing orange-brown pustules and reducing photosynthesis.
symptoms: Premature leaf drying in severe infection
treatment: Apply rust-effective fungicide at early disease onset
Monitor upper canopy during susceptible growth stages
prevention: Avoid late sowing where rust pressure is high

Wheat___Healthy.description: No rust or blight features identified on the wheat leaf.

Wheat___Yellow_Rust.description: A rust disease causing yellow stripe-like pustules along wheat leaves.
symptoms: Rapid spread in cool moist conditions
treatment: Use recommended fungicides for stripe rust
